criticalIssues.title: Missing Employee Management API - No User/Employee CRUD Operations
criticalIssues.description: The system has NO API endpoints for creating, updating, or deleting employees/supervisors/admins. The only way to onboard users is through Excel bulk import (/api/excel/upload), which lacks proper authorization checks.

locations.issue: POST /api/excel/upload endpoint accepts authenticated user (any role) but should be admin-only

criticalIssues.impact: Admins cannot manage employees programmatically. Only bulk Excel import available. No way to deactivate or update employees without direct DB access.
criticalIssues.recommendation:
Implement comprehensive employee management API with proper authorization:
- POST /api/admin/employees (create employee)
- PUT /api/admin/employees/:id (update employee)
- DELETE /api/admin/employees/:id (soft delete)
- GET /api/admin/employees (list all)
- POST /api/admin/supervisors/:id/assign-employees (assign employees to supervisor)

criticalIssues.title: Frontend Routes Not Role-Protected - Unauthorized Access to Dashboards
criticalIssues.description: Frontend routes for supervisor dashboard (/supervisor) and security dashboard (/security) are NOT protected by role-based access. Any authenticated user can directly visit these URLs and see the components.

locations.issue: No ProtectedRoute or role-based route guards. Routes conditionally render in Navbar but URL can be visited directly.
No role verification before rendering sensitive supervisor data
No role verification before rendering security events/spoof attempts

criticalIssues.impact: An employee can navigate to /supervisor or /security URLs and potentially see/manipulate supervisor/security dashboards. Data API calls may fail, but UI is exposed.
criticalIssues.recommendation:
Implement role-based route protection:
1. Create ProtectedRoute component that verifies user.role
2. Apply to /supervisor and /security routes
3. Add role checks in dashboard component useEffect
4. Redirect unauthorized users to /dashboard

criticalIssues.title: Supervisor Scope Validation Ambiguous - May Access Non-Assigned Employees
criticalIssues.description: Supervisor attendance history query allows filtering by department OR specific employee without validating that supervisor is assigned to that employee. Scope is ambiguous: supervisor sees their department by default but can request any employee's data.

locations.issue: if (targetEmployeeId && isSupervisor) allows any supervisor to request any employee. Should verify supervisor_id match OR admin role only.

criticalIssues.impact: Supervisors may be able to view attendance of employees they don't supervise. Privilege escalation risk.

criticalIssues.title: Mock Data Generators Exposed in Production Code
criticalIssues.description: Development-only mock data generators (mock-routes.js, testSetup.ts) and development server (dev-server.js) exist in production codebase. If DEV mode is accidentally enabled or mock routes are mounted, system returns fake data.

locations.issue: Complete mock API routes with fake attendance, leave, security data
Development server with mocked database and Redis connections
Mock user data (admin, supervisor, employee) and fake attendance records

criticalIssues.impact: If development mode is enabled in production, system returns fabricated data instead of real attendance records. Critical data integrity issue.
criticalIssues.recommendation:
1. Remove mock-routes.js and dev-server.js from production builds
2. Remove VITE_ENABLE_MOCK_LOGIN check from LoginPage
3. Remove testSetup.ts or mark as test-only
4. Use environment-based code splitting to exclude dev code from production builds

criticalIssues.title: No Leave Approval Audit Trail - Missing Approval Tracking
criticalIssues.description: Leave request approval/rejection endpoints don't consistently log who approved, when, or why. The audit_logs table exists but is not used for leave approvals.

locations.issue: PUT /request/:id/approve updates leave_requests but doesn't log approval to audit_logs
PUT /request/:id/reject updates leave_requests but doesn't log rejection reason to audit_logs

criticalIssues.impact: No compliance audit trail. Cannot determine who approved/rejected leaves, when, or for what reason. Critical for compliance and dispute resolution.
criticalIssues.recommendation:
Add audit logging to approval/rejection endpoints:
```javascript
await logAuditEvent({
  actorEmployeeId: req.user.employeeId,
  action: 'leave.request.approve',
  resourceType: 'leave_request',
  resourceId: String(leaveId),
  ipAddress: req.ip,
  userAgent: req.headers['user-agent'],
  details: { approvedLeaveType, approvalDate },
});
```

architecturalGaps.title: Missing Employee-to-Supervisor Assignment Management
architecturalGaps.description: The employees table has supervisor_id foreign key, but there are NO endpoints to assign employees to supervisors. Supervisor-employee relationships can only be set via Excel import or direct SQL.

architecturalGaps.title: No Leave Balance or Leave Policy Enforcement
architecturalGaps.description: The system does not track or enforce leave balances. No leave_policy table defines annual leave allowance, carry-over rules, or leave type limits. Supervisors can approve unlimited leave requests.
architecturalGaps.impact: Employees could theoretically take unlimited leave. No way to enforce company leave policies.
architecturalGaps.recommendation: Create leave_policy table with: leave_type, annual_days, carry_over_days, requires_approval_after_days

architecturalGaps.title: No Configurable Work Timings - Hardcoded or Missing
architecturalGaps.description: System has no work_timings table or API. Office hours, break times, and work schedules appear hardcoded or missing. The late arrivals check uses hardcoded '09:00:00' time.
architecturalGaps.impact: Cannot configure different work timings for different employees/departments. Late arrival detection is inflexible.

architecturalGaps.title: No Department Management or Configuration
architecturalGaps.description: Employees have department field but there's no department management API or department_config table. Departments are free-form strings with no validation.

architecturalGaps.title: Hardcoded Office Location - Not Configurable per Employee
architecturalGaps.description: office_locations table exists with 'Main Office' hardcoded. No API to add/modify locations. Geofence validation uses single office location.

locations.issue: INSERT INTO office_locations (name, latitude, longitude) VALUES ('Main Office', 40.7128, -74.0060)

architecturalGaps.title: No Employee Onboarding Workflow
architecturalGaps.description: No endpoints for initial setup: password enrollment, face registration, MFA setup. Employees cannot self-onboard. Depends on admin to Excel import then manually contact employee.

architecturalGaps.title: No Supervisor-Employee Hierarchy Validation
architecturalGaps.description: System accepts any supervisor_id in employees table without validating that the supervisor is actually a supervisor role. Employees could theoretically be assigned to employee-role users as 'supervisors'.
architecturalGaps.recommendation: Add constraint: supervisor_id must reference employee with role='supervisor' or role='admin'

missingTables.name: work_timings
missingTables.purpose: Configure work hours, break times, schedule types per employee/department

missingTables.name: leave_policy
missingTables.purpose: Define leave types, annual allocations, carry-over rules, approval requirements

missingTables.name: leave_balance
missingTables.purpose: Track employee leave balance per type per year

missingTables.name: supervisor_assignments
missingTables.purpose: Track which employees are assigned to which supervisors (explicit many-to-one relationship)

missingRoutes.impact: Required for flexible work hour configuration

missingComponents.name: ProtectedRoute
missingComponents.impact: Frontend routes not protected from unauthorized access

missingComponents.name: AdminPanel
missingComponents.impact: No UI for admin user management

missingComponents.name: LeavePolicyManager
missingComponents.impact: Cannot manage leave policies through UI

fakeDataSources.impact: If imported and used, returns fabricated attendance, leave, and security data

data.attendance: Mock check-in/check-out times hardcoded to current time
data.leave: Fake leave requests with hardcoded status 'approved'
data.security: Mock spoof attempts and security events

fakeDataSources.impact: If run in production, completely mocks database and Redis
Can login with any employeeId, auto-detects role from employeeId string
Could be used in tests but should not be in production

permissionEnforcementGaps.currentBehavior: Any authenticated user can visit URLs; navbar conditionally shows links
permissionEnforcementGaps.risk: Employees can directly access supervisor/security dashboards

permissionEnforcementGaps.issue: POST /api/excel/upload not restricted to admin role
permissionEnforcementGaps.currentBehavior: Any authenticated user can upload and create employees

permissionEnforcementGaps.issue: Supervisor can request attendance for any employee without verification
permissionEnforcementGaps.risk: Supervisors can spy on other departments

permissionEnforcementGaps.issue: Leave request detail endpoint checks: employee OR admin OR supervisor (no assignment check)
permissionEnforcementGaps.currentBehavior: lr.supervisor_id = $2 OR $3::text = 'admin' - but no 'assigned supervisor' check
permissionEnforcementGaps.risk: Any supervisor can view any leave request

permissionEnforcementGaps.issue: Security events returned without supervisor scope filtering
permissionEnforcementGaps.risk: Supervisors see security events for employees they don't manage

scopeValidationIssues.query: SELECT ar.* FROM attendance_records ar JOIN employees e WHERE targetEmployeeId AND isSupervisor
scopeValidationIssues.issue: Allows supervisor to request any employee's attendance without verifying supervisor_id match
scopeValidationIssues.risk: Supervisors can access attendance of non-supervised employees
scopeValidationIssues.fix: Check employee.supervisor_id == req.user.id before returning data

scopeValidationIssues.query: SELECT se.* FROM security_events se LEFT JOIN employees e
scopeValidationIssues.issue: Returns all security events for any supervisor; no department or assignment filter
scopeValidationIssues.fix: Filter WHERE se.employee_id IN (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE supervisor_id = $1)

scopeValidationIssues.query: SELECT lr.* FROM leave_requests lr WHERE lr.supervisor_id = $2 OR $3::text = 'admin'
scopeValidationIssues.issue: Ambiguous: supervisor sees their leaves OR all leaves if assigned as supervisor somewhere
scopeValidationIssues.risk: Leave query returns all leaves where supervisor_id matches, but no validation that supervisor is actually assigned
scopeValidationIssues.fix: Validate supervisor assignment before returning leave data

scopeValidationIssues.query: SELECT * FROM login_logs ll JOIN employees e ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT $1
scopeValidationIssues.issue: Returns all login logs for any supervisor; no scope to assigned employees
scopeValidationIssues.risk: Supervisors see authentication attempts for entire company

scopeValidationIssues.query: SELECT e.department FROM employees e WHERE e.department = $${paramCount}
scopeValidationIssues.issue: Department filter works but assumes all supervisors can see entire departments; no manager field
scopeValidationIssues.risk: Multiple supervisors might manage same department, or supervisor from different department might access
scopeValidationIssues.fix: Use proper supervisor_id relationship or department_manager field, not free-form department string

keyFindings.rbac_implementation: 3-level hierarchy (admin > supervisor > employee) is implemented in code but NOT enforced consistently across API and frontend
keyFindings.admin_capabilities: Admins can create employees only via Excel import; no API for CRUD operations. Cannot assign supervisors or manage departments.
keyFindings.supervisor_capabilities: Supervisors can view 'team' attendance but scope validation is weak - they can request any employee's data. No verification of actual assignment.
keyFindings.employee_restrictions: Employees correctly restricted to own data in most endpoints, but frontend routes are not protected.
keyFindings.leave_approval_audit: NO audit trail for leave approvals/rejections. Only manual 'approval_date' field in leave_requests table.
keyFindings.data_integrity: Mock data generators present in production code. DEV mode can return fabricated data if accidentally enabled.
keyFindings.database_schema: Missing critical tables: work_timings, leave_policy, leave_balance, department_config, supervisor_assignments. Office location hardcoded.
keyFindings.api_routes: No employee management endpoints. No leave policy management. No work timings configuration. No supervisor assignment endpoints.
keyFindings.frontend_components: No role-based route guards. AdminPanel and management components missing.
keyFindings.permission_middleware: RBAC middleware exists but not applied consistently. Excel upload not restricted. Frontend routes not protected.
keyFindings.frontend_routing: Routes render conditionally in navbar but URLs accessible directly without role verification.
keyFindings.scope_queries: Multiple queries lack proper supervisor-employee relationship validation.

exposures: Employees can bulk import fake employees
Supervisors can access data from other departments/supervisors
No leave policy enforcement - unlimited leave possible
No audit trail for leave approvals
Mock data can be returned as real data
complianceGaps: Missing department/work timing configurations required for compliance
Scope validation insufficient for multi-supervisor environment

recommendedActions.action: Implement ProtectedRoute component and gate /supervisor, /security routes by role
Add supervisor assignment validation to all supervisor-accessible queries
Remove mock-routes.js and dev-server.js from production builds
Implement employee management API with proper authorization
Add audit logging to all leave approvals/rejections
Create missing tables: work_timings, leave_policy, leave_balance
Implement supervisor assignment verification in scope queries
